reviews.why: True homonym. Child A's evidence (AAA, evA3) is a restaurant order-channel metric: delivery's share of restaurant orders vs dine-in. Child B's evidence (BBB evB2, CCC evB3) is a logistics shipping-mode metric: parcel volumes shifting between ground/air freight and next-day courier service tiers. Object differs (restaurant order share vs parcel volume mix), scope differs (restaurant sales channels vs freight transport modes), mechanism differs (channel shift by diners vs mode/service-tier shift in parcel shipping) — all three union checks fail. New names drawn only from evidence words: "restaurant"+"delivery channel mix" → restaurant_delivery_channel_mix; "parcel delivery"+"mix" → parcel_delivery_mix.

Both occurrences name the identical reusable cause: the market price of crude oil. Object/metric match (crude oil price in both quotes: "higher crude oil prices..." evA4 / "crude oil price increases..." evB4); scope matches (market-wide commodity price, no grade or geography qualifier in either side); mechanism matches (crude price changes raising fuel input costs). The differing downstream effects (AAA distribution fuel costs vs BBB jet fuel/diesel costs) are company-specific impact channels that per DriverOntology R2 live outside driver_name, not evidence of different drivers. oil_price is also the R5 familiar canonical form for this cause.

Each side has exactly one ref, and both quotes are semantically empty: child_a (AAA) "traffic was a factor this period" and child_b (BBB) "traffic trends were mixed". Neither identifies the object (store/foot/web/network traffic), scope, or mechanism, so none of the three union checks (same object/metric, same scope, same mechanism) can be verified — SAME cannot be asserted. DIFFERENT also fails: there is no evidence of two distinct meanings, and the quotes contain no specific nouns from which more-specific names could be coined without inventing words (banned by DriverOntology R4/R10). Fail-close: park as UNCLEAR.
